senses.text: "present in the (consecrated) bread," 1540s, from Church Latin impanatus, past participle of impanare "to embody in bread," from assmiliated form of in- "in, into" (see in- (2)) + panis "bread," from PIE root *pa- "to feed" (see food). Related: Impanation (1540s), from Medieval Latin impanationem.
